svg.text: 2. Initialise result with the first interval
• If it overlaps the last in result (current.start ≤ last.end), extend last.end
Output: list of disjoint intervals covering the same union as the input.
Time: O(n log n) for the sort. Space: O(n) for the output.
• Input is a list of [start, end] pairs
• Question asks for: merged ranges, total covered length, count of distinct groups
• Or: insert an interval, find conflicts, find free time, count overlap at a point
• Or: schedule meetings, allocate rooms, deduplicate booking ranges
If two intervals can overlap and the question is about their relationship,
the merge-intervals pattern (or a sweep-line variant) is the answer.
Sort by start, walk once, merge or append. Three lines of logic, dozens of problems.
